title: Name resolution not working when a DNS container is started
body:
### Issue Description

Hi All,

I have the following situation. I have multiple containers running on the same network, these containers can find and communicate with each other. However as soon as I start a container with a DNS server, all communication breaks.

Any ideas how to solve that? 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman network create --subnet=192.168.89.0/24 --gateway=192.168.89.1 --dns=192.168.122.1 --driver=bridge proxy
2. podman pod create --name pm --network proxy -p 81:80
3. podman pod create --name db --network proxy -p 3306:3306
4. podman pod create --name dns -p 3000:3000 -p 53:53/tcp -p 53:53/udp --network proxy
5. podman run --name adguardhome --pod=dns   --restart unless-stopped    -v /home/podman/adguard/work:/opt/adguardhome/work    -v /home//podman/adguard/conf:/opt/adguardhome/conf    -d adguard/adguardhome
6. podman run -dt --restart=always --name mariadb --pod=db --secret mysql_root_password -v /home/podman/mariadb/data:/var/lib/mysql -v /home/podman/mariadb/config:/etc/mysql/conf.d -e MARIADB_AUTO_UPGRADE=1 -e MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD_FILE=/run/secrets/mysql_root_password mariadb:latest
7. podman run -dt --restart=always --pod=pm --name pma --secret mysql_root_password -e PMA_HOST=db -e PMA_PORT=3306 -e MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD_FILE=/run/secrets/mysql_root_password phpmyadmin/phpmyadmin:latest




### Describe the results you received

As soon as I stop Adguard communication between pm and db work, once started communication fails

### Describe the results you expected

Communication continued to work when a DNS server is started

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

I also tried using docker-compose (my preferred way) to create the network and spin the containers up, but same results

comments.body:
If you start a dns server yourself it will obviously cause a port conflict with aardvark-dns.
You either have to add you primary interface ip to the port mapping, e.g. `-p 192.168.1.1:53:53/tcp -p 192.168.1.1:53:53/udp` change the ip with the correct one.

Or alternatively you have to change `dns_bind_port` to some high port in containers.conf.

Thanks for the reply. Changing the dns_bind_port didn't work, first suggestion did.

P.s. with this pull would aardvark not use a different port then 53? [https://github.com/containers/netavark/pull/323](url)

Did you stop all your containers before changing the port, aardvark-dns must be stopped in order for this field to take effect. 53 is the default and will only be changed if you set it in containers.conf

Thanks.

Yes I rebooted my system. Will containers.conf also effect networks created by docker-compose?
